title: Iphiona grantioides
taxonomy: Iphiona grantioides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the broader category of daisies and similar plants. Within the Asteraceae family, Iphiona grantioides is further classified into the tribe Inuleae. This tribe is a group of flowering plants known for their small, composite flowers. Iphiona grantioides is native to the Mediterranean region and surrounding areas.

intro: Iphiona grantioides, also known as Egyptian helichrysum, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the management of digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Iphiona grantioides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Iphiona grantioides is often used in traditional medicine and is available in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
